headline: Mistral AI: French Ministry of Armed Forces 3-year defense framework deal (notified Dec 16 2025, announced Jan 8 2026) — Mistral accepted military operational AI integration covering all armed services, intelligence units, and research agencies. CSEM/CBRN safety study (Euronews May 8 2025) was already factored into April 16 assessment (verified in April 16 proposal evidence record); no double-counting. Net assessment: French defense framework is a SYS structural event qualitatively similar to US Pentagon cohort scope. Sovereign-data-control architecture is somewhat more constrained than US 'any lawful purpose' framing, but embedding into intelligence processing across all armed services is a material BND/SYS signal. Composite 46.9 → 45.6 (-1.3). Sub-threshold delta. Functional band sustained (40-60). Recommend confirm with notation.
key_evidence: Dec 16 2025: France's Ministry of Armed Forces notified Mistral AI of 3-year defense framework deal.
Jan 8 2026: Public announcement of Mistral defense contract.
Scope: all armed services, intelligence units, research agencies (CEA, ONERA, naval hydrographic units).

Architecture: sovereign software stack — foundation models, enterprise assistants, document processing, custom agents — deployed via on-premises, private-cloud, or self-hosted; not on foreign or commercial cloud.
Coordination via AMIAD (Agence ministérielle pour l'intelligence artificielle de défense).
CSEM/CBRN Enkrypt AI study (Euronews May 8 2025) — VERIFIED already cited in April 16 proposal evidence; not double-counted in this cycle.
Cumulative baseline sustained: April 16 -29.5 composite downgrade (76.4 → 46.9) from CSEM/CBRN findings; Thorn partnership; EU AI Act compliance preparation.

evidence.finding: France deploys Mistral AI across military to accelerate operational decision-making.
Mistral AI wins French military deal.
Mistral AI landed military contracts while US rivals face public backlash.
Mistral signs defense deal as Europe pushes for sovereign AI.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.75 unchanged. No new awareness-mechanism evidence in window.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 → 2.9 (-0.1). B3 Scope Clarity / B4 Risk-Boundary: embedding model architecture into intelligence processing across all armed services materially expands the operational scope of Mistral's deployments. Sovereign-data architecture is a partial mitigant but does not address the scope question itself. Modest dock.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.25 → 3.15 (-0.1). S2 Mission Coherence / S3 Distributional Awareness: defense framework integration is a structural pivot from open-source civilian-AI framing. AMIAD oversight is institutional but does not change the structural-deployment question. Modest dock.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.5 unchanged. Mistral's pre-existing sovereign-AI / European-alternative framing was always inclusive of defense applications; no integrity gap detected — the deal is consistent with stated mission framing in a way the US Pentagon-cohort deals were not. INT not docked.

math_check.validator_check: Drift well within 2.0pt per-cycle ceiling. Sub-threshold delta. Functional band sustained (40-60).

csem_study_verification.issue: Scanner flagged: verify whether the CSEM/CBRN Enkrypt AI study (cited in scanner news_summary as Euronews May 2025) was already factored into the April 16 baseline.
csem_study_verification.verification: Confirmed: the April 16 change-proposal evidence record explicitly cites the Euronews May 8 2025 URL and the 60x CSAM finding as the primary basis for the -29.5 composite downgrade. The CSEM/CBRN finding is NOT new evidence in this cycle and is not double-counted.

negativeSignals: Defense framework integration across all French armed services, intelligence units, research agencies; intelligence-processing scope; structural pivot from open-source civilian framing.
positiveSignals: Sovereign-data architecture (on-premises, private-cloud, self-hosted) more constrained than US 'any lawful purpose' framing; AMIAD institutional oversight; less public ethics controversy than US peers — peer-relative posture; consistent with European sovereign-AI mission framing (no INT inconsistency).
sector_context: AI Labs sector cluster: Mistral defense framework + US Pentagon cohort (Microsoft, Amazon, Google, OpenAI) + Anthropic exclusion form the May 2026 'AI for state defense operations' landscape. Mistral is distinguished by sovereign-data architecture and pre-existing European-AI mission framing. Cross-reference: pending change proposals from April 16 are now confirmed/applied per published index update.
open_actions_resolved: French military contract scored under BND -0.1 / SYS -0.1 modest dock
CSEM study double-count check verified — no double-counting
open_actions_carried: Monitor for first operational deployment evidence under AMIAD framework
Watch: any safety-evaluation disclosure on intelligence-processing applications
Watch: EU AI Act compliance update — May 2026 reporting cadence
